events.name: Electro Hunt
events.description: Electro Hunt is a technical clue-based event focused on electrical and electronics concepts. Participants must solve clues and progress through stages within a time limit. It tests subject knowledge, logical thinking, and speed. Each clue leads to the next challenge, making it engaging and competitive. Accuracy and quick responses are essential to win.
rules: Only one round will be conducted
No external help or devices allowed
Participants must follow the given clues sequentially

events.name: Circuit Crafting
events.description: Circuit Crafting is a hands-on technical event where participants build electrical circuits within a limited time. Teams will be given components and a problem statement to design and test a functional circuit. It evaluates practical knowledge, accuracy, and time management. Participants must ensure proper connections and working output. The event emphasizes real-time problem-solving skills.
rules: No mobile phones or external help allowed
Components will be provided at the venue
Circuit must be completed within the given time

events.name: TECHTALK ARENA (paper presentation)
events.description: TechTalk Arena is a platform for presenting innovative ideas and research in emerging technologies. Participants will deliver a structured presentation on topics related to EEE such as power systems, renewable energy, or electric vehicles. The event evaluates technical knowledge, presentation skills, and clarity. Participants should effectively communicate their ideas to judges. Confidence and originality play a key role
rules: Topics must be related to EEE domain

events.name: INVENTRA(project expo)
events.description: Inventra is a project exhibition where participants showcase innovative ideas through working models or prototypes. It focuses on solving real-world problems using electrical and electronics concepts. Teams must clearly explain the working, application, and technical aspects of their project. Creativity, feasibility, and presentation skills will be evaluated by judges. It is an opportunity to demonstrate both technical knowledge and innovation.
rules: Working model or prototype is mandatory
Each team must explain their project clearly to judges
Projects should be related to Electrical/Electronics or real-world applications

events.name: Brain and Chalk
events.description: Brain and Chalk is a creative drawing and guessing game. One participant draws a given word while teammates try to guess it within the time limit. It tests creativity, communication, and teamwork. The event is fast-paced and entertaining, requiring quick thinking and expression. Correct guesses earn points for the team.
rules: 1 minute to draw and 1 minute to guess
No speaking, lip-syncing, or gestures allowed
Only drawing is permitted for communication

events.name: Beat the Clock 30
events.description: Beat the Clock 30 is a fun and fast-paced event consisting of multiple small tasks to be completed within a strict time limit. Each round challenges participants with different activities that test speed, coordination, and quick thinking. The team that successfully completes the maximum number of tasks within the given time will be declared the winner. It is designed to create an energetic and enjoyable experience for all participants.
rules: Event consists of multiple short tasks
Each task must be completed within the given time limit
No use of mobile phones or external help
Winner will be decided based on maximum tasks completed in time

events.name: Lab lockdown
events.description: The Lab Lockdown event consists of three exciting rounds focused entirely on electronics. In the first round, participants will take a mini digital electronics test to check their basic knowledge. The second round involves a lab circuit finding challenge, where participants must identify and analyze circuits in the lab. In the final round, teams will design and build a mini electronic circuit project, testing their creativity and practical skills.
rules: Participants must follow lab safety rules
Electronic components will be provided by the organizers
Mobile phones are not allowed during the event
Participants must complete the tasks within the given time
Any form of malpractice will lead to disqualification

events.name: Line following Robot
events.description: The Line Follower Robot Competition challenges participants to design and program an autonomous robot that can follow a predefined line track using sensors and a microcontroller. Each team will consist of 1-3 members. The robot must complete the track accurately and in the shortest possible time without human control. This event encourages skills in robotics, programming, electronics, and teamwork.
rules: Participants must bring their own robot and components
Robot must be completely autonomous (no human control)
Robot should follow the given line track correctly
Robot completing the track in the shortest time will score higher
Any damage to the track will lead to disqualification

events.name: TECHTALK ARENA (paper presentation)
events.description: A Paper Presentation event here participants can showcase their innovative ideas and research in the field of electronics. Each team must consist of 1 to 3 members. The topics should be strictly related to electronics and emerging technologies. Participants will present their ideas using slides and explain the concept, working principle, applications, and future scope. This event encourages students to improve their technical knowledge, presentation skills, and creativity.
rules: Topics must be related to electronics and emerging technologies
Abstract submission required before the event day
Presentation should cover concept, working principle, applications, and future scope

events.name: INVENTRA(project expo)
events.description: Exhibit your innovative project with a working prototype or demo. Impress the judges with your creativity, technical depth, and real-world impact.
rules: Must have a working demo or prototype
Project report must be submitted beforehand
Judging based on innovation, feasibility, and execution

events.name: Chess & Carrom
events.description:
CHESS
Prove your tactical brilliance in this individual knockout tournament! The games strictly follow the official FIDE rules, including the touch-move rule. Compete in a fast-paced 10+5 minute time-controlled format where victory is claimed by checkmate, opponent resignation, or time out. Will you claim the board?

CARROM
Partner up for this thrilling black & white coin doubles tournament! Compete in a knockout format until one team successfully pockets all their coins along with the Queen. There are no time limits—only pure skill. Bring your own striker, report slightly before your match, and battle it out for exciting cash prizes!
rules: Rules: Games will follow official rules of FIDE
Touch-Move Rule: If a player touches a piece, it must be moved
Winning: Checkmate, opponent resignation, or time over
Decision: Referee/Organizers decision will be final
Winning Rule: Team that pockets all coins along with the Queen first wins
Striker: Players may use their own striker
Reporting: Teams must report 15 minutes before the match
Venue & Time: Will be announced in the WhatsApp group
Participation: Open to all branches, boys and girls
Prizes: Cash Prize for Winner & Runner-up | Participation Certificates for all

events.name: RAPID FIRE QUIZ
events.description: Test your speed and knowledge in this fast-paced quiz! Teams will face a series of rapid-fire questions to score points and prove their expertise.
rules: Participants can take part in teams (2 members).
Each team will be asked a series of rapid questions.
Time limit: 30 seconds – 1 minute per round.
Participants must answer quickly without using mobile phones or internet.
If a team cannot answer, the question may be passed to the next team.
The team with the highest score at the end of the quiz wins.
In case of a tie, a tie-breaker round will be conducted.

events.name: TECHTALK ARENA (paper presentation)
events.description: A paper presentation involves preparing a research paper and explaining it to an audience using tools such as slides, charts, and diagrams. The presenter explains the topic, objectives, methodology, results, and conclusions clearly so that the audience can understand the research work.
rules: Topics must be strictly related to Mechanical engineering and emerging technologies
Abstract submission required before the event day

events.description: Computer-Aided Design (CAD) is the use of computer software to create, modify, analyze, and optimize a design. It is widely used in engineering, architecture, product design, and manufacturing. (SOFTWARE: SOLID WORKS)

It is commonly used in mechanical engineering training, manufacturing industries, and repair works.

events.name: Find The Song
events.description: A song is a piece of music with words that are intended to be sung. It combines lyrics, rhythm, melody, and sometimes musical instruments to create a complete musical work. Guess the song based on the given clues!
rules: Points awarded for speed and accuracy

events.name: Mystery Bag Madness
events.description: Participants pick a bag containing a random object and must perform a funny act, pitch a product, or complete a creative task using it. The team with the most creative and entertaining performance wins.
rules: Acts must be creative and entertaining
Obscene or offensive content is strictly prohibited

events.name: Quick start
events.description: Quick Start is an idea presentation event where participants pitch their startup concepts. It evaluates innovation, feasibility, and business understanding. Participants must clearly explain their idea using a presentation. The event encourages entrepreneurial thinking and problem-solving skills. Clear communication and originality are essential for scoring.
rules: Idea should be clearly explained and structured

events.name: Component Identification
events.description: Component Identification is a technical event where participants recognize electrical and instrumentation components and state their functions. It tests practical knowledge and observation skills within a limited time. Participants must quickly identify components and understand their real-time applications. Accuracy and speed are key factors for scoring. The event focuses on core fundamentals of instrumentation.
rules: Each component must be identified within 1 minute
Answers should be written on paper

events.description: TechTalk Arena provides a platform to present innovative ideas in instrumentation and emerging technologies. Participants will explain concepts related to IoT, sensors, automation, robotics, or smart systems. The event evaluates technical knowledge, clarity, and presentation skills. events.name: Reels making
events.description: Reels Making is a creative event where participants produce short videos based on given topics. It focuses on creativity, storytelling, and editing skills within a limited time. Teams must plan, shoot, and present engaging content. The event encourages originality and fun expression. Final reels will be evaluated based on creativity and presentation.
rules: Reel must be completed within 45 minutes
Use of AI for scripting or editing is not allowed

events.name: Brand detection
events.description: Brand Detection is a fun-based event where participants identify logos within a limited time. It tests memory, observation, and quick thinking skills. Participants must recognize brands accurately and write their answers. The event is simple yet engaging, making it enjoyable for everyone. Speed and correctness determine the winner.
rules: Logos must be identified within the given time
Answers should be written on paper

events.name: Promptify X
events.description:
Event Overview: Prompt Engineering Challenge 

 Prompt Engineering is the art of crafting precise and effective instructions to guide AI models toward accurate, high-quality results. In this challenge, participants will be tested on their ability to observe intricate visual details and translate them into clear, descriptive prompts for AI image generation. 

 Round 1: The Foundation Basics Briefing (5 Mins): A quick dive into the core principles of prompt engineering, covering structure, style descriptors, and technical parameters. 

 Knowledge Check (10 Mins): A fast-paced Q&A session consisting of 10 questions to test your understanding of AI interaction. 

 Round 2: Visual Re-creation 
 The Challenge: Participants will be shown a specific reference image. 
 The Goal: Write a single, comprehensive prompt that instructs the AI to recreate the image as accurately as possible. 

 Judging Criteria: Success is measured by how many elements from the original picture—such as composition, lighting, subjects, and color palette—appear in the AI-generated result.
rules: 2 rounds: Basics, Creative Prompting, and Prompt Battle
Use of external tools is prohibited

events.name: code X paradise
events.description:
This is Code arena:

 Round 1: Reverse Coding Think you can work backward? We give you the output; you figure out the logic. Decipher the pattern and write the code to match it. Speed and logic are your only allies.

 Round 2: HackerRank Contest The ultimate showdown. Solve a series of algorithmic challenges on the HackerRank platform. Accuracy and efficiency are the names of the game climb the leaderboard before the clock runs out.

events.name: Rhythm Rush
events.description:
~Start music game description : 

Start Music is a fun game where players identify Tamil cinema songs using small clues. The clues may be a BGM (background music), a line of lyrics, or a picture related to the song. Players listen or look at the clue and try to guess the correct song as quickly as possible.

The player who correctly identifies the song first earns points. This game tests players’ memory, listening skills, and knowledge of Tamil movie songs, making it exciting and entertaining for music lovers. 

•Round 1 – BGM Round:Players listen to a short background music (BGM) clip from a Tamil movie song and guess the correct song.

•Round 2 – Picture Round:Players see a picture related to a song (such as a movie scene, actor, or object) and identify the song connected to it.

•Round 3 – Missing Lyrics Round:Players are given a line from a song with missing words and must fill in the correct lyrics
rules: 3 rounds: Melody, Rapid Fire, and Finals
No use of phones or Shazam-like apps

events.name: IPL Auction
events.description:
This IPL Acution:

The event consists of three exciting rounds based on the Indian Premier League (IPL) to test participants’ cricket knowledge, observation skills, and strategic thinking.

Round 1: IPL Quiz Participants answer questions related to IPL teams, players, records, and history. This round tests the cricket knowledge and quick thinking of the teams.

Round 2: Historical moments of IPL Teams identify IPL players, team logos, stadiums, or memorable moments from pictures shown on the screen. This round checks visual recognition and cricket awareness.

Round 3: IPL Auction Teams act as IPL franchise owners and use a virtual budget to bid for players and build their team. The winner is decided based on team balance, player selection, and budget management.Overall, the event is designed to be fun, interactive, and competitive, encouraging teamwork while celebrating the excitement of the Indian Premier League
rules: Each team gets a virtual budget of ₹80 Crores
No communication between teams during bidding

events.name: Algowar (Code War)
events.description:
The Code Battle contest consists of three rounds: MCQ, Coding Challenge, and Rapid Fire Coding, designed to test participants’ programming knowledge and problem-solving abilities.

The first round is an MCQ quiz containing questions from Java and Python programming, data structures, and basic algorithms, covering fundamental programming concepts, syntax, and output analysis.

The second round is a coding challenge with problems similar to those on the LeetCode platform, focusing on array and string-based problems with varying difficulty levels.

The final round is a rapid fire coding round where participants must quickly solve simple programming problems within a limited time. This round evaluates speed, accuracy, and logical thinking in coding
rules: Languages allowed: C, C++, Java, Python

events.name: Connection
events.description: Connection Game is a fun non-technical event that tests participants’ thinking ability, observation skills, and teamwork. Teams must find the common connection between given words, pictures, or clues within the given time. The event consists of three rounds with increasing difficulty.

events.name: Brand It Now!!
events.description: Brand It Now is a fun and fast-paced event where participants create creative advertisements for the given product on the spot. Using imagination, humor, and confidence, contestants turn simple or unusual products into entertaining and convincing ads. Get ready for quick thinking, creativity, and lots of fun!
